Dashboard Archive: 2026-05-12 Home
About This Dashboard
What It Does
Long-term momentum ranker scoring 700+ tickers on returns from 1 month to 10 years, with relative performance vs SPY over 1y/5y/10y. Same scoring engine as the short-term ranker but optimized for longer investment horizons and secular trends.
Today's Reading
# Market Analysis: momentum-ranker-long LINC dominates today's rankings with a score of 95.6 and rank #1, displaying exceptional long-term momentum across all timeframes—particularly its 10-year return of 2,349% and 5-year return of 652%, both vastly outpacing SPY by 2,091% and 574% respectively. The stock shows clean momentum signals with all four moving average crossovers intact (sma_all: true) and healthy ratio progression from 1-month to 5-year returns (ratios of 3.35 to 4.65), though the ratio from 6-month to 1-year drops to 0.77, suggesting some deceleration in recent performance. In contrast, CVE ranks second but exhibits a critical long-term weakness: its 10-year return of 101% trails SPY by 157%, indicating this energy stock has significantly underperformed the broad market over the decade despite strong recent performance (126% 1-year return). Both stocks pass all technical moving average filters and maintain 4 out of 5 ratio thresholds, but LINC's superior relative strength and positive outperformance across all benchmark comparisons place it in a fundamentally different strength category for long-term investors.
For educational and informational purposes only. Not financial advice. Past performance does not guarantee future results.
